annotations.label: Confidence interval (frequentist)

anchor.snippet: a confidence interval ( CI ) is a range of values which is likely to contain

annotations.note: No notion of confidence interval appears in Mathlib (grep for `confidence` returns nothing).

annotations.label: Confidence interval for a parameter

anchor.snippet: A confidence interval for the parameter

annotations.note: Mathlib has no parametric statistics framework defining confidence intervals.

annotations.label: Conservative confidence interval

anchor.snippet: the confidence interval is called conservative

annotations.note: Without a definition of confidence interval, no notion of `conservative` exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Central limit theorem method for CI

anchor.snippet: using the asymptotically standard normal quantity

annotations.note: The CLT itself is formalized as `tendstoInDistribution_inv_sqrt_mul_sum_sub`, but its use to build a confidence interval is not.

annotations.label: CI for the mean of a normal distribution
annotations.note: Mathlib provides `gaussianReal` but does not formalize confidence intervals for its mean.

annotations.label: Pivotal quantity has Student's t distribution
annotations.note: Student's t distribution is not formalized in Mathlib (no `StudentT` or analogous declaration).

annotations.label: 95% prediction interval for a twenty-sided die

anchor.snippet: there are exact 95% prediction intervals for rolling a twenty-sided die

annotations.note: No notion of prediction interval appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Optimal 50% confidence procedure for uniform location
annotations.note: Mathlib has no confidence procedure framework, so this example is absent.

annotations.label: Steiger's confidence procedure for ω²

anchor.snippet: the confidence interval shrinks and can even contain only the single value

annotations.note: ANOVA effect-size ω² and Steiger's confidence procedure are not formalized in Mathlib.
